name: Paul Butterfield
abstract: Paul Vaughn Butterfield (December 17, 1942 – May 4, 1987) was an American blues harmonica player, singer, and bandleader. After early training as a classical flautist, he developed an interest in blues harmonica.

concerts.label: Odetta, Butterfield Blues Band, and Albert King
Rick Danko/ Paul Butterfield/ Kate and Anna McGarrigle

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: It ended up being called Better Days and we did two albums and then I did some other albums with him after that band broke up.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Levy
about_quotes.evidence: I fell in love with the sound of the blues harmonica and all the Chicago guys, you know Paul Butterfield, James Cotton, Little Walter
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Levy interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 11, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Mandel
about_quotes.evidence: And the blues stuff from Chicago, even on the white side, Mike Bloomfield and Paul Butterfield. So that interested me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Mandel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: My kids were, even my stepchildren were like totally into Mick Jagger and Paul Butterfield and all those.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002
